perturbation.original_text: For the purpose s of this Agreement, “Confidential Information ” shall mean any information disclosed by Oceaneering , whether in writing, orally, visually or otherwise, including but not limited to business plans, contractual, engineering, financial, sales, marketing and operational information, product specifications, technical data , trade secrets , know -how, ideas and concepts of Oceaneering or third parties . Confidential Information excludes, however, information which : (i) is or becomes known or available to Recipient without restriction from a source other than Oceaneering with a legal right to disclose th e same to Recipient; (ii) is, or without violating the terms of this Agreement becomes , generally available to the public ; or (iii) is developed by Recipient independently of the information disclosed hereunder.
perturbation.changed_text: For the purpose s of this Agreement, “Confidential Information ” shall mean any information disclosed by Oceaneering , whether in writing, orally, visually or otherwise, including but not limited to business plans, contractual, engineering, financial, sales, marketing and operational information, product specifications, technical data , trade secrets , know -how, ideas and concepts of Oceaneering or third parties. Confidential Information also includes information that is already publicly known or independently developed, so long as Oceaneering has disclosed it to the Recipient. Confidential Information excludes, however, only information which is restricted from a source other than Oceaneering with a legal right to disclose th e same to Recipient.
perturbation.explanation: This change broadens the definition of 'Confidential Information' to include publicly known or independently developed information, contradicting the original exclusions. This creates uncertainty because Section 2 restricts the use and disclosure of 'Confidential Information,' and now the recipient is unsure whether they are obligated to protect information they already knew or developed. It specifically contradicts the original exclusions set forth in section 1 regarding (ii) and (iii).
perturbation.contradicted_text: With respect to all Confidential Informati on disclosed hereunder, Recipient agrees that from and after the date of this Agreement, Recipient shall not: a. use the Confidential I nformation except for purposes of its business relationship with Oceaneering (the “ Authorized Purpose ”); or b. disclose the Confidential I nformation to any third party except : (i) for directors, officers, managers, employees , consultants , contractors and professional advisors of Recipient (collectively its “Representatives”) who need to know the Confidential Information for the Authorized P urpose and who are subject to an existing obligation to, or enter into an agreement with, Recipient not to disclose Confidential Information ; and (ii) as may be authorized in writing in advance by an officer of Oceaneering .
perturbation.justification: The changed text broadens the definition of 'Confidential Information' to include information that is already publicly known or independently developed. This directly contradicts the original text which specifically excluded such information. The contradicted text defines obligations regarding the use and disclosure of 'Confidential Information'. By including publicly known or independently developed information, the changed text creates a direct conflict with the contradicted text, because the recipient is now obligated to protect information that would have originally been excluded, causing uncertainty and unenforceability.

perturbation.original_text: With respect to all Confidential Informati on disclosed hereunder, Recipient agrees that from and after the date of this Agreement, Recipient shall not: a. use the Confidential I nformation except for purposes of its business relationship with Oceaneering (the “ Authorized Purpose ”); or b. disclose the Confidential I nformation to any third party except : (i) for directors, officers, managers, employees , consultants , contractors and professional advisors of Recipient (collectively its “Representatives”) who need to know the Confidential Information for the Authorized P urpose and who are subject to an existing obligation to, or enter into an agreement with, Recipient not to disclose Confidential Information ; and (ii) as may be authorized in writing in advance by an officer of Oceaneering .
perturbation.changed_text: With respect to some Confidential Informati on disclosed hereunder, Recipient agrees that from and after the date of this Agreement, Recipient shall not: a. use the Confidential I nformation except for purposes of its business relationship with Oceaneering (the “ Authorized Purpose ”); or b. disclose the Confidential I nformation to any third party except : (i) for directors, officers, managers, employees , consultants , contractors and professional advisors of Recipient (collectively its “Representatives”) who need to know the Confidential Information for the Authorized P urpose and who are subject to an existing obligation to, or enter into an agreement with, Recipient not to disclose Confidential Information ; and (ii) as may be authorized in writing in advance by an officer of Oceaneering .
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'all Confidential Information' to 'some Confidential Information' introduces ambiguity. The recipient is now uncertain which subset of confidential information is subject to the restrictions in this section, directly contradicting the intent of sections such as section 3, which imposes obligations of compliance regarding confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient shall require its Representatives who receive any Confidential Information to comply with the terms and conditions of this Agreement and Recipient shall be responsible for their compliance herewith . Recipient shall use at least the same degree of care to prote ct the confidentiality and ensure the proper use of the Confidential Information as Recipient uses with respect to its information of a similar kind or nature, but in no event less than reasonable care .
perturbation.justification: The original text states 'all Confidential Information', creating a clear obligation for the Recipient. The changed text modifies this to 'some Confidential Information', introducing ambiguity as to which confidential information is subject to the agreement. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which requires the Recipient to ensure that its representatives comply with the terms of the agreement with respect to 'any Confidential Information' they receive. By limiting the scope of the initial clause to 'some' confidential information, it creates uncertainty and a contradiction as to which confidential information the recipient is responsible for protecting under the terms of the agreement as referenced in the Contradicted Text.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient shall use at least the same degree of care to prote ct the confidentiality and ensure the proper use of the Confidential Information as Recipient uses with respect to its information of a similar kind or nature, but in no event less than reasonable care .
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient shall use some degree of care to prote ct the confidentiality and ensure the proper use of the Confidential Information, but in no event less than minimal care .
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'at least the same degree of care...but in no event less than reasonable care' to 'some degree of care...but in no event less than minimal care,' the level of protection required is significantly lowered. This contradicts the spirit of the agreement, which aims for robust protection of Oceaneering's Confidential Information, and also potentially contradicts the clause in section 9 relating to remedies if this minimal care is not kept.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient agrees that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for breach of this Agreement and that, in addition to all other remedies, Oceaneering shall be entitled to specific performance and injunctive or other equitable relief as a remedy for such breach.
perturbation.justification: The changed text lowers the required degree of care for confidential information from 'at least the same degree of care...but in no event less than reasonable care' to 'some degree of care...but in no event less than minimal care'. This contradicts the contradicted text, which states that 'money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for breach of this Agreement and that, in addition to all other remedies, Oceaneering shall be entitled to specific performance and injunctive or other equitable relief as a remedy for such breach.' The original text ensured a higher degree of care, making the breach more serious and justifying equitable relief. However, the lowered standard in the changed text weakens the justification for such remedies, potentially making money damages sufficient, and thus contradicting the clause specifying equitable relief. This also adheres to the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction', as the shift introduces interpretation challenges regarding adequate protection and proper remedies, making enforcement unclear.

perturbation.original_text: Oceaneering grants no rights in or to the Confident ial Information . All Confidential Information shall remain the sole property of Oceaneering.
perturbation.changed_text: Oceaneering grants a limited right in or to the Confident ial Information, specifically the right to use the information internally for evaluation purposes. All Confidential Information shall remain the sole property of Oceaneering.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by granting the Recipient a 'limited right' to use the Confidential Information, while simultaneously stating that 'All Confidential Information shall remain the sole property of Oceaneering'. The introduction of a limited right creates ambiguity about the scope and limitations of Oceaneering's sole ownership, potentially leading to disputes over usage rights. This directly contradicts the clause in Section 2, which limits usage to business purposes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: With respect to all Confidential Informati on disclosed hereunder, Recipient agrees that from and after the date of this Agreement, Recipient shall not: a. use the Confidential I nformation except for purposes of its business relationship with Oceaneering (the “ Authorized Purpose ”); or b. disclose the Confidential I nformation to any third party except : (i) for directors, officers, managers, employees , consultants , contractors and professional advisors of Recipient (collectively its “Representatives”) who need to know the Confidential Information for the Authorized P urpose and who are subject to an existing obligation to, or enter into an agreement with, Recipient not to disclose Confidential Information ; and (ii) as may be authorized in writing in advance by an officer of Oceaneering .
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a 'limited right' to use the Confidential Information, while the contradicted text in Section 2 explicitly states that the Recipient shall not use the Confidential Information except for the Authorized Purpose (business relationship with Oceaneering). The introduction of a limited right for 'evaluation purposes' creates a direct contradiction because it allows for use outside of the 'Authorized Purpose' defined in Section 2, which is a clear conflict within the document based on the Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction definition. A reasonable reader would find these sections incompatible.

perturbation.original_text: Any p rovision to the contrary notwithstanding, Recipient ’s obligations under this Agreement are subject to any disclosure requirement of law, regulation or legal process , but only to the extent of such requirement . Recipient shall promptly notify Oceaneering of any such requirement, cooperate fully with Oceaneering’s requests to prevent or minimize the effect of such disclosure , and make all reasonable efforts to have such disclosures placed under a protecti ve order or other wise obtain confidential treatment of the Confidential Information .
perturbation.changed_text: Any p rovision to the contrary notwithstanding, Recipient ’s obligations under this Agreement are subject to any disclosure requirement of law, regulation or legal process , but only to the extent of such requirement . Recipient may notify Oceaneering of any such requirement, and may cooperate with Oceaneering’s requests to prevent or minimize the effect of such disclosure .
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall promptly notify' to 'may notify' and removing 'make all reasonable efforts' weakens the recipient's obligation to protect Confidential Information when faced with a legal disclosure requirement. This creates a contradiction with Section 3, which places a positive requirement of protecting the confidentiality.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient shall require its Representatives who receive any Confidential Information to comply with the terms and conditions of this Agreement and Recipient shall be responsible for their compliance herewith . Recipient shall use at least the same degree of care to prote ct the confidentiality and ensure the proper use of the Confidential Information as Recipient uses with respect to its information of a similar kind or nature, but in no event less than reasonable care .
perturbation.justification: The changed text weakens the Recipient's obligation to protect confidential information. The original text uses 'shall promptly notify' and 'make all reasonable efforts,' creating a clear duty. The changed text uses 'may notify' and removes 'make all reasonable efforts,' making the notification and effort optional. This contradicts the contradicted text, which states 'Recipient shall use at least the same degree of care' and 'Recipient shall be responsible for their compliance herewith,' imposing a positive obligation to protect confidentiality. The weakening of the obligation in the changed text directly conflicts with the requirement for care and responsibility in the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: Upon Oceaneering’s written request, Recipient shall promptly : (i) deliver to Oceaneering and cease to use all Confidential Information in Recipient’s (including its Representatives’) possession, custody or control ; or (ii) destroy the same and delete all electronic records containing the Confidential Information, provided that Oceaneering may require Recipient to certify in writing such destruction and deletion.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon Oceaneering’s written request, Recipient shall use its best efforts to: (i) deliver to Oceaneering all Confidential Information in Recipient’s (including its Representatives’) possession, custody or control; or (ii) destroy the same and delete all electronic records containing the Confidential Information, provided that Oceaneering may require Recipient to certify in writing such destruction and deletion. Recipient is not required to cease using the Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'shall promptly' to 'shall use its best efforts' and adding 'Recipient is not required to cease using the Confidential Information', the obligation to return or destroy the information is weakened, and the recipient's ongoing use is permitted. This introduces ambiguity and directly contradicts the implied requirement of ceasing usage in the original text as well as section 2 which restricts the usage of confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: With respect to all Confidential Informati on disclosed hereunder, Recipient agrees that from and after the date of this Agreement, Recipient shall not: a. use the Confidential I nformation except for purposes of its business relationship with Oceaneering (the “ Authorized Purpose ”); or b. disclose the Confidential I nformation to any third party except : (i) for directors, officers, managers, employees , consultants , contractors and professional advisors of Recipient (collectively its “Representatives”) who need to know the Confidential Information for the Authorized P urpose and who are subject to an existing obligation to, or enter into an agreement with, Recipient not to disclose Confidential Information ; and (ii) as may be authorized in writing in advance by an officer of Oceaneering .
perturbation.justification: The changed text weakens the obligation to return or destroy confidential information by changing 'shall promptly' to 'shall use its best efforts' and explicitly states that the recipient is 'not required to cease using the Confidential Information'. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that the recipient 'shall not: a. use the Confidential Information except for purposes of its business relationship with Oceaneering', creating a strong in-text contradiction regarding the permitted use of confidential information after a request from Oceaneering.
